Module_1_Identity.1. Age / Generation: 31 years old (Born 1993 - a birth year he frequently appends to his handles)
Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Austin, Texas, USA. Deeply embedded in the local tech meetup and craft brewery culture.
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Middle class. Freelance web developer and active open-source contributor with a public GitHub profile full of dotfiles, side projects, and unscrubbed commit histories.
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Apolitical tech-optimist. Strong advocate for maker culture, right-to-repair, and open-source software.
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Casual, enthusiastic, and collaborative. Uses heavy developer jargon, Discord slang, and frequently overshares personal anecdotes in forum replies.
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: English (American, heavily influenced by internet and tech culture).
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Single, renting an apartment, and heavily invested in expensive hobbies. He obsessively posts updates about his 3D printing projects, mechanical keyboards, and his two cats, 'Boba' and 'Pixel', across Reddit, Instagram, and Twitter.

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): Android (Google Pixel 7, 2 years old). Rooted, frequently tinkers with developer settings, and heavily integrated with Google ecosystem.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): Custom-built desktop running Arch Linux for development (with public dotfiles) and Windows 11 for gaming. Primary browser is Firefox with uBlock Origin, but heavily relies on built-in browser password auto-fill for convenience.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: High theoretical tech knowledge but poor practical execution due to convenience bias. Uses a VPN (Mullvad) for specific traffic but ignores basic password hygiene, reusing predictable variations (e.g., 'Boba1993!', 'PixelPrint93') across countless minor forums, webshops, and GitHub. Rarely clears cookies.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: Extensive and promiscuous cloud syncing. Backs up VS Code settings, browser profiles, and dotfiles to GitHub (occasionally leaking minor API keys in commit histories). Uses Google Drive for all personal photos and project backups.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: High fatigue. Thousands of unread Discord mentions and promotional emails. Only actively monitors GitHub issue alerts, Reddit replies, and 3D printing Discord channels. Likely to miss automated security login alerts buried in his inbox.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Types exceptionally fast on a custom split ortholinear mechanical keyboard. Prone to specific muscle-memory transpositions (e.g., 'teh', 'cna'). Uses perfect Markdown formatting everywhere, even in casual messaging, but frequently skips capitalization in quick, enthusiastic replies.

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: High. Constantly exploring new tech, hobbies, and maker projects (3D printing, custom keyboards). Eager to share and document everything publicly.
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: Low. While technically skilled, he prioritizes convenience over security. He exhibits poor digital hygiene, reuses highly predictable password patterns across platforms, and ignores the tedious maintenance of proper OPSEC.
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: High (Digital). Highly active and gregarious in online communities, Discord servers, and GitHub issues. Habitually overshares personal details, including pet names and daily routines.
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: High. Collaborative and helpful in open-source spaces. Wants to be seen as a friendly, approachable member of the maker community.
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: Low. Generally optimistic and unbothered by potential digital threats. Lacks the baseline paranoia necessary to maintain good security practices, assuming 'it won't happen to me'.
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: Ego. He derives immense self-worth from his identity as a skilled developer, maker, and helpful community member. He wants his projects, dotfiles, and setups to be admired.
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: Vice/Relationships (Parasocial/Pets). His deep emotional attachment to his cats ('Boba' and 'Pixel') and his need for validation in tech communities are prime sources for predictable password generation and dictionary attacks.
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: Losing his digital reputation, having his GitHub account compromised, or being mocked by peers for his deeply flawed personal security practices despite his professional status.
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: Deep imposter syndrome regarding his actual technical competence, specifically the shame of being 'hacked' due to elementary mistakes like leaking API keys in dotfiles or reusing his cats' names as passwords.

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Night owl (sleeps 3:00 AM - 10:00 AM). Late-night hours are prime time for rushed, unreviewed GitHub commits where he is most likely to accidentally push .env files or hardcode credentials.
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Highly erratic freelance schedule. Mixes client work with open-source contributions throughout the day and night, leaving a continuous, heavily timestamped digital trail that maps his exact online presence.
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: Works from home, so there is no daily commute. Short, sporadic offline windows occur when driving to local Austin tech meetups or craft breweries in the early evening (6:00 PM - 8:00 PM).
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: Minimal variance. Weekends blur into weekdays, though Saturdays see a spike in hobby-related posts (3D printing failures, custom keyboard builds) and Instagram stories featuring his cats, Boba and Pixel, continually feeding OSINT scrapers with fresh password permutations.
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Heavy doomscrolling and rapid-fire forum posting from bed upon waking (10:00 AM - 11:00 AM) and before sleep (1:00 AM - 3:00 AM). Frequently overshares in Discord servers during these low-inhibition periods.
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Rarely truly offline except when sleeping. 'Focus blocks' (1:00 PM - 4:00 PM) merely shift his footprint from social media to intensive GitHub pushes and Stack Overflow queries. True zero-footprint time is strictly between 4:00 AM and 9:30 AM.

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Single, lives alone with his two cats, Boba and Pixel, who function as his primary domestic focus. His parents live in suburban Dallas; he frequently uses his mother's maiden name and his childhood street name as answers to legacy security questions, both of which are easily scrapeable from his lightly-secured Facebook profile.
Module_5_Relational.31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts: 1. @alextx_makes (Alex): A fellow Austin-based open-source developer and co-admin of a local maker Discord server. 2. Sarah Jenkins: His primary freelance client contact; they share a Trello board and Google Drive where he occasionally leaves hardcoded API keys in shared documents. 3. 'MechKeebsATX' (Discord Group): The collective hivemind of his local mechanical keyboard meetup group where he overshares personal setup details, routine schedule, and location data.
Module_5_Relational.32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer: Chloe, his college ex-girlfriend. While completely absent from his current public life and social media, her name, combined with his birth year ('chloe1993!'), remains the foundational string for several of his legacy platform passwords found in 2018-era breach dumps—a pattern the credential stuffer easily identifies and uses to guess his modern variations.
